25 But in all Israel there was none to be so much. praised as Absalom for his beauty; from the sole of his foot even to the crown of his head there. was no blemish in him.

"Abshalom exalted himself with his hair," etc. Our Rabbis were taught Abshalom rebelled [against his father] through his hair, as it is said (II. Sam. 14, 25) And like Abshalom there was no man as handsome in all Israel, so that he was greatly praised. From the sole of his foot up to the crown of his head, there was no blemish on him.
